blocks.text: DTC P0243: West gate control SOL.V. circuit failure

The waste gate control SOL.V. is attached to the positive pressure piping of the west gate valve diaphragm of the turbocharger. The PGM-FI ECU uses a linear solenoid to regulate the positive pressure applied to the wastegate valve diaphragm, bypassing the exhaust pressure to the exhaust pipe and varying the exhaust pressure hitting the turbine. As a result, turbine rotation on the compressor side is variable to control boost pressure to the intake manifold. If the return signal does not change in response to the West Gate Control SOL.V. output signal from the PGM-FI ECU for a set period of time or longer, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores a DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
West Gate When the return signal does not change in response to the control SOL.V. output signal for 2 seconds or longer.

items.text: Disconnection of cord (WGS line) between PGM-FI ECU and West Gate Control SOL.V.
Ground fault in code (WGS line) between PGM-FI ECU and West Gate Control SOL.V.
Disconnection of cord (power supply line) between west gate control SOL.V. and fuse box
Internal failure of West Gate Control SOL.V.
Step on the accelerator pedal several times in the P or N position until the engine speed [engine rpm] exceeds 2,500 rpm.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
